'''Luke "The General" Patten''' (born 9 January 1980) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and NRL match official. A Junior Kangaroo and [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country New South Wales]] representative {{rlp|fb}} he played for the [[Illawarra Steelers]], [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in Australia and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]]. Patten won the [[2004 NRL Premiership]] with the Bulldogs.

Patten played in the famous 1999 Grand Final between the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and the [[Melbourne Storm]] played in front of a record crowd of 108,000 people at the Homebush Olympic Stadium.

In 2001, he joined the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and quickly made the fullback position his own. In 2004 he played in every game of the Canterbury-Bankstown premiership winning season which finished with a 16–13 victory Grand Final win over the [[Sydney Roosters]].

A two-time winner of the clubs best and fairest in 2006 & 2007, Patten became the club's heart and soul. A back injury ended his 2008 campaign but he returned in 2009 to help a rejuvenated club reach the preliminary final against the [[Parramatta Eels]]. Unfortunately he copped a head knock early in the game in a huge blow to the Bulldogs hopes.

At the end of the [[2010 NRL season]] he signed a 3-year deal to join the [[Salford City Reds]] in the English Super League. In his last season for the club (2012) he was voted the clubs best and fairest.

Upon returning to Australia in 2013 he joined the NRL match officials as a video referee. In all he officiated in over 400 first class games before leaving the role.

In 2015 he was voted as fullback, in the team of the decade (2000-2010) for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].

In 2020 he accepted a role with the NRL Match Review Committee.

==Background==
Patten was born in [[Bulli, New South Wales]], Australia of [[English people|English]] descent.

==Playing career==
An Illawarra junior, Patten acquired the nickname "General" because of his surname's similarity to that of [[George S. Patton|General Patton]].

Patten made his first grade debut for Illawarra in round 3 of the [[1998 NRL season]] against [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] at [[WIN Stadium]].  Patten played in Illawarra's final game as a stand-alone entity when they played against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]] in round 24 1998 at [[WIN Stadium]].  Patten scored two tries in a 25–24 loss.  At the end of 1998, Illawarra formed a joint-venture partnership with [[St. George Dragons|St. George]] to become [[St. George Illawarra]].

He played for [[St. George Illawarra]] at fullback in the [[1999 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Melbourne Storm]]. He moved to the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in 2001.

Patten played at fullback for Canterbury in their [[2004 NRL grand final]] victory over cross-city rivals, the [[Sydney Roosters]]. As 2004 [[NRL]] premiers, Canterbury faced [[Super League IX]] champions, [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2005 World Club Challenge]]. Patten played at {{rlp|fb}} and scored a try in Canterbury's 32–39 loss.

Patten was selected for Country in the [[City vs Country Origin]] match on 8 May 2009.<ref>{{cite news|work=Fox Sports|location=Australia|title=Blues halfback duel in two as Mitchell Pearce misses selection|first=Todd|last=Balym|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121205020103/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=2012-12-05|date=2009-05-03|access-date=2009-05-03}}</ref> In 2009, Patten was 19th man for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Blues]] in Game 3.

On 27 July 2010 it was confirmed that Patten would be moving to the [[Super League]], linking up with English club [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] for 3 years from the 2011 season.<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/news/sport/luke-patten-to-link-with-salford-reds/story-e6frg7mf-1225897369781| first=Margie| last=McDonald| title=Luke Patten to link with Salford Reds| date=2010-07-27| work=The Australian}}</ref>

Patten and [[Trent Barrett]] were the joint last remaining former [[Illawarra Steelers]] players to play in the [[NRL]].<ref>{{cite news|first=Nick|last=Walshaw|title=Sacrifice wins Fitzy's spot|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/sport/nrl/story/0,26799,23693633-5006066,00.html|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=2008-05-14|access-date=2008-08-26}}</ref>

===Career highlights===
* ''Junior Club:''  [[Corrimal Cougars]]
* ''First grade debut:'' Round 3, 1998 – Illawarra v [[Newcastle Knights]], [[WIN Stadium]].
* ''NSW Game 3, 18th Man – 2009''.
* ''100th First Grade Try, 30th July 2010.''.

===Awards===
* Canterbury ''Player of the Year'' – 2006.
* Canterbury ''Player of the Year'' – 2007.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|title=Patten Bulldog of the Year|url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,22528716-5012650,00.html|access-date=2007-10-27|date=2007-10-03}}</ref>

==Refereeing career==
Patten first showed interest in becoming a referee in 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/luke-patten-set-to-become-nrl-referee/story-e6frfgbo-1226188845751|title=Luke Patten set to become NRL referee|website=Heraldsun.com.au|access-date=2016-04-12}}</ref> In 2013 he became the first ex-player from the NRL era to officiate in a first grade grand final when he served as [[Video referee (rugby league)|video referee]] for the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/patten-to-make-history-in-grand-final/tabid/10874/newsid/74923/default.aspx|title=Patten to make history in Grand Final|date=October 2013 |publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=2016-04-12}}</ref>
